Lecture2/02_00a_Discussion.notes: §2.1 opens with localization of rings; follows introduction

Lecture2/02_00b_Discussion.notes: §2.2 on ideals in localizations; builds on §2.1 localization setup

Lecture2/02_01_Theorem.notes: Theorem 2.1 (prime ideal correspondence) stated in context of §2.2

Lecture2/02_02_Remark.notes: Explicitly references 'an immediate consequence of (2)', where equation (2) is in 02_00b_Discussion

Lecture2/02_02a_Discussion.notes: Uses Theorem 2.1: 'prime ideals of A_p are in bijection with prime ideals of A contained in p'

Lecture2/02_03_Remark.notes: References 'the notation in (3)', where equation (3) is in 02_02a_Discussion

Lecture2/02_04_Example.notes: Uses localization at a prime (02_02a) and 'A is a PID, so A_p is a PID' from Remark 2.2

Lecture2/02_05_Example.notes: 'As in the previous example' — explicitly references Example 2.4

Lecture2/02_06_Proposition.notes: Proposition 2.6 uses module localization framework from §2.3

Lecture2/02_06a_Discussion.notes: Lean file imports only Mathlib; ideal localization = ideal extension proved from Mathlib alone

Lecture2/02_07_Corollary.notes: Lean file imports 02_06_Proposition; corollary of Proposition 2.6

Lecture2/02_08_Example.notes: Lean file imports only Mathlib; Z = ∩ Z_(p) in Q proved from Mathlib alone

Lecture2/02_08a_Discussion.notes: Explicitly references 'Proposition 2.6 and Corollary 2.7 are powerful tools'

Lecture2/02_09_Proposition.notes: Lean file imports only Mathlib; Dedekind domain characterization proved from Mathlib alone

Lecture2/02_10_Definition.notes: Lean file imports Mathlib + SutherlandNumberTheoryLecture1 (external); no internal Lecture 2 dependencies

Lecture2/02_11_Corollary.notes: Lean file imports only Mathlib; PID is Dedekind domain proved from Mathlib alone

Lecture2/02_12_Remark.notes: Lean file imports only Mathlib; PID/UFD/Dedekind relationships proved from Mathlib alone

Lecture2/02_12a_Discussion.notes: Lean file imports only Mathlib; integral closure of Dedekind domains proved from Mathlib alone

Lecture2/02_12b_Discussion.notes: §2.5 introduction on fractional ideals; sets up standing assumptions

Lecture2/02_13_Definition.notes: Fractional ideal definition; uses §2.5 setup (noetherian domain, fraction field)

Lecture2/02_13a_Discussion.notes: Elaborates on Definition 2.13; explains how fractional ideals generalize ideals

Lecture2/02_14_Lemma.notes: Characterizes finitely generated A-submodules of K; uses fractional ideal setup

Lecture2/02_15_Remark.notes: 'Lemma 2.14 gives an alternative definition' — explicitly references Lemma 2.14

Lecture2/02_16_Corollary.notes: Direct consequence of Lemma 2.14; every fractional ideal has form (1/a)I

Lecture2/02_17_Definition.notes: Principal fractional ideal; extends fractional ideal concept

Lecture2/02_17a_Discussion.notes: Operations on fractional ideals: sum, product, quotient; uses Definition 2.17

Lecture2/02_18_Lemma.notes: Proof explicitly uses 'by Lemma 2.14' twice; also uses ideal quotient from 02_17a

Lecture2/02_19_Definition.notes: Invertible fractional ideal; follows from quotient being a fractional ideal

Lecture2/02_20_Lemma.notes: Invertibility criterion via A ÷ I; uses Definition 2.19 and quotient concept

Lecture2/02_20a_Discussion.notes: Preview of Dedekind domain invertibility result; follows Lemma 2.20

Lecture2/02_21_Example.notes: Illustrates Lemma 2.20 failure: computes A ÷ I and shows I(A ÷ I) ⊊ A

Lecture2/02_21a_Discussion.notes: §2.6 introduction; recaps fractional ideals and explicitly references 'Lemma 2.20'

Lecture2/02_22_Definition.notes: Ideal group I_A; defined using invertible fractional ideals from §2.6 intro

Lecture2/02_22a_Discussion.notes: Principal fractional ideals P_A form subgroup of I_A; references both concepts

Lecture2/02_23_Definition.notes: Ideal class group cl(A) = I_A / P_A; uses both groups from preceding items

Lecture2/02_24_Example.notes: DVR ideal class group is trivial; uses Definition 2.23

Lecture2/02_25_Remark.notes: Dedekind domain is UFD iff cl(A) trivial; uses ideal class group definition
